description:
You are a Site Reliability Engineer who just fixed a tiny bug in an uptime-monitoring
utility that lives in a local Git repository located at

/home/user/uptime-dashboard

Current repository status
─────────────────────────
1.  /home/user/uptime-dashboard/VERSION  
    • single-line text file that currently contains exactly:
      1.4.2

2.  /home/user/uptime-dashboard/monitor.py  
    • A short Python script that defines the constant  
      TIMEOUT = 3  
      (the too-small timeout was the root cause of recent false positive
      downtime alerts).

3.  /home/user/uptime-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md – already follows
   “Keep a Changelog” + Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 and presently starts
   with the header section shown below (the other older sections follow—
   do **not** remove or touch them):

   ```
   # Changelog

   ## [Unreleased]
   ### Fixed
   - None yet
   ```

Goal
────
After applying the bug-fix you must perform a PATCH semantic version bump
(from 1.4.2 → 1.4.3) and update the changelog.  
The automated grader will look for **both** the code change *and*
the versioning metadata.

Required final state
────────────────────
A. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/monitor.py  
   • The line containing the constant must now read exactly  
     TIMEOUT = 5

B. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/VERSION  
   • Must contain exactly one line:  
     1.4.3  
     (end with a trailing newline).

C. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
   • The top of the file (line 1 downward) must be **exactly**:

     ```
     # Changelog

     ## [1.4.3] - 2023-12-01
     ### Fixed
     - Adjusted timeout handling in monitor.py to prevent false downtime alerts (issue #57).

     ## [Unreleased]
     ### Fixed
     - None yet
     ```

     After that, keep the rest of the pre-existing historical
     changelog entries unchanged.

     Notes about formatting that the grader will verify:
       1. Two blank lines after “# Changelog”.
       2. A single blank line between every heading block
          (mirrors the example above).
       3. The date must be written exactly “2023-12-01”.
       4. The bullet line must begin with “- ” (a dash and a space).

D. Git repository  
   • A commit must encapsulate the three file changes mentioned above.
     (The grader will simply check that `git status` is clean.)

Logging requirement
───────────────────
Before finishing, write a short text log named

/home/user/uptime-dashboard/bump.log

containing three lines in the following fixed format so the grader can
cross-check what you did:

1. VERSION: <new version>
2. FILE_CHANGED: monitor.py
3. CHANGELOG_TOP: <first heading after “# Changelog”>

It must therefore look exactly like:

VERSION: 1.4.3
FILE_CHANGED: monitor.py
CHANGELOG_TOP: 1.4.3 - 2023-12-01

(Do not add extra whitespace or blank lines.)

Constraints & hints
───────────────────
• Do NOT create or delete any other files.  
• You do not have root privileges; stay under /home/user.  
• Stick to the precise spelling, capitalization, punctuation and
  whitespace shown above—tests are strict.  
• A clean `git status` at the end is mandatory.

When everything is done, simply exit; no separate submission step is
needed—the grader will inspect the filesystem and Git history.
truth:
Initial filesystem snapshot created by the test harness
-------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/uptime-dashboard
├── VERSION          -> contains: "1.4.2\n"
/home/user/uptime-dashboard/monitor.py
      → somewhere in the first 10 lines contains: "TIMEOUT = 3"
/home/user/uptime-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md
      → starts exactly with:
         "# Changelog\n\n## [Unreleased]\n### Fixed\n- None yet\n\n## [1.4.2] - 2023-11-20\n### Added\n- Initial uptime monitoring capability.\n"
/home/user/uptime-dashboard is already a git repository with a clean
working tree.

Expected final state that the grader will validate
--------------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/monitor.py
   • Single exact replace: "TIMEOUT = 5" (nothing else changed).

2. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/VERSION
   • Content must be "1.4.3\n"

3. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md
   • Top 11 lines must be exactly:

     # Changelog

     ## [1.4.3] - 2023-12-01
     ### Fixed
     - Adjusted timeout handling in monitor.py to prevent false downtime alerts (issue #57).

     ## [Unreleased]
     ### Fixed
     - None yet

   • The remainder of the old changelog (starting from the existing
     “## [1.4.2] – 2023-11-20 ...”) must stay byte-for-byte identical.

4. /home/user/uptime-dashboard/bump.log
   • Exact 3-line content:
     VERSION: 1.4.3
     FILE_CHANGED: monitor.py
     CHANGELOG_TOP: 1.4.3 - 2023-12-01

5. Git
   • `git status --porcelain` must output nothing.
   • `git log -1 --pretty=%s` will be captured but not validated for
     content; only that there is at least one new commit compared with
     the starting point.

Permissions
-----------
All paths under /home/user are owned by the invoking user and writeable.
